name: Apple Pie in Cheddar Crust
recipeIngredient: 7 cups Apples (use tart cooking apples such as Macs or Granny Smith's)
recipeInstructions:
1. Core, pare, and chop the apples.
2. CHEDDAR CRUST: Combine the flour, cheese, and salt in a bowl. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the shortening until coarse crumbs form. Sprinkle with 5-6 tablespoons water and using a fork, toss the crumbs until a dough is formed. Press the dough firmly into a ball.
3. Roll out the larger half of the dough to a 13-inch circle. Line a 9-inch pie plate with the pastry, trimming the edges to 1/2-inch beyond the rim of the pie plate. Place in the pie plate and gently work from the center, spreading the pastry until it covers the bottom of the pie plate and then lay the rest of the pastry over the rim and trim.
4. Combine the apples, sugar, flour, and cinnamon in a bowl, mixing well.
5. Arrange the apple mixture in the pastry-lined pie plate. Roll out the remaining pastry to an 11-inch circle. Gently fold into quarters and cut steam slits in the folds. Unfold the crust and place on top of the filling, trimming the crust to 1-inch beyond the rim of the pie plate.
6. Fold the top crust under the lower one and flute to form a ridge around the edge of the pie plate.
7. Combine the egg yolk and water, then brush the mixture over the top crust and rim.
8. Bake in a 400 degree F oven for 45-50 minutes or until apples are tender and the crust is golden brown. Cool on a wire rack until slightly warm and serve with Vanilla Ice Cream if desired.
